topic: Consumer financial product comparison for a purchase decision
reader: buyer comparing two products and asking which risks deserve attention
use_context: A decision-support comparison that weighs fees, incentives, lock-in, and user fit without implying personalized financial advice.
required_claims: who each option appears to suit
prohibited_overclaims: does not claim the cheapest option is always best
does not recommend a product as a fiduciary adviser
does not ignore fees hidden outside the headline price

source_packets.title: Product terms sheet
source_packets.summary: Lists pricing, renewal behavior, cancellation terms, and the most salient product features.

findings.statement: The lowest advertised price does not include recurring service fees.
findings.decision_note: This should drive the cautionary language in the comparison.
failure_modes: presenting the headline price as the full cost
misunderstandings: assuming advertised price equals out-the-door cost

findings.statement: Cancellation terms include a notice window that can create lock-in.
findings.decision_note: Highlight lock-in separately from product quality.
failure_modes: ignoring exit friction when summarizing the product
misunderstandings: assuming the product is easy to exit at any time

source_packets.title: Independent comparison note
source_packets.summary: Compares the same products across usage scenarios and calls out where the lower-cost option may be less suitable.

findings.statement: The cheaper option can become more expensive for users who exceed the included usage threshold.
findings.decision_note: This supports a scenario-based rather than price-only comparison.
failure_modes: flattening usage thresholds into a single average price
misunderstandings: assuming the cheapest plan stays cheapest for heavy users

findings.statement: Feature parity is incomplete, so the decision depends on which workflow matters most.
findings.decision_note: This belongs in the final comparison table or recommendation section.
failure_modes: treating nominally similar products as interchangeable
misunderstandings: assuming all missing features are minor
